articles.title: Four in five landlords unprepared for Renters’ Right Act
articles.summary: Four in five landlords (82%) are concerned about the impact of the Renters’ Rights Act, with one in two (44%) very concerned. Meanwhile, three-quarters (75%) of landlords say regulation has made them less likely to grow their portfolios, and half (49%) are planning to sell or reduce their stake within two months.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Major Concern for Landlords**: 82% of landlords express concerns over the Renters’ Rights Act, with 44% being very worried about its potential impact.  
- **Portfolio Shrinking**: 75% feel that regulations discourage growth, leading to half planning to sell or reduce their property holdings in the next two months.

articles.title: Energy efficiency and EPC ratings become higher focus for tenants
articles.summary: Landlords must consider how to improve EPC ratings to attract tenants, even ahead of the new requirement to upgrade rental properties to a C rating by 2030. It comes as energy efficiency of properties has gained even greater focus as tenants look to save money after the energy price hikes caused by the Iran War.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords need to enhance EPC ratings to attract tenants, especially with the upcoming 2030 mandate for rental properties to achieve at least a C rating.
- Rising energy costs, driven by the Iran War, have heightened tenant interest in energy-efficient homes reflecting a shift in rental priorities.

articles.title: Planning reform fails to inspire confidence – Landbay
articles.summary: Fewer than a quarter of the UK’s landlords feel planning reform will positively impact their business.
articles.ai_summary:
- Less than 25% of UK landlords believe upcoming planning reforms will benefit their operations.
- A significant lack of confidence in planning reform raises concerns for the property market.

articles.title: Landlords warned a hidden leak could cost thousands
articles.summary: With more than half (58%) of hidden leaks originating outside a property, landlords must remember to focus on the maintenance of pipework outside as well as inside the property, leak detection specialist Aspect has warned.
articles.ai_summary:
- Over 58% of hidden leaks in properties come from external pipework, highlighting a major oversight for landlords.  
- Leak detection experts emphasize the importance of maintaining both indoor and outdoor plumbing to prevent costly repairs.

articles.title: As RRA deadline looms, 5 top tips for landlords
articles.summary: From 1 May 2026, the Renters’ Rights Act will implement some of the biggest changes to the Private Rented Sector in England and Wales. The Act will aim to balance landlord and tenant rights and will impose new requirements and regulatory oversight on landlords.
articles.ai_summary:
- The Renters’ Rights Act, effective from 1 May 2026, will introduce significant changes to tenant and landlord rights in the Private Rented Sector in England and Wales.  
- Landlords must prepare for new regulatory requirements and oversight by following key guidelines to ensure compliance.

articles.title: 84% of landlords not ready for RRA – survey
articles.summary: More than eight in ten (84%) of landlords are unprepared for the Renters’ Rights Act go-live on May 1, with 58% at high risk of non-compliance according to new figures.
articles.ai_summary:
- A staggering 84% of landlords are unprepared for the Renters’ Rights Act set to take effect on May 1.  
- Over half (58%) of landlords may face significant risks of non-compliance with the new regulations.

articles.title: Expansion no longer default response to rising demand as rental growth slows
articles.summary: Landlords are restructuring portfolios and financing in response to changing rental demand, rather than focusing on expansion, with the ONS announcement of a slowing in rental growth showing the need to ensure portfolios are right.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords are pivoting from expansion to restructuring portfolios and financing as rental growth slows, reflecting a strategic shift in response to market changes.  
- The ONS highlights a deceleration in rental growth, prompting landlords to reassess and optimize their property portfolios rather than pursuing new acquisitions.

articles.title: Landlords blamed for pushing first-time and local buyers out
articles.summary: Landlords and investors have been blamed for pushing first-time buyers and local buyers out of the market, according to a new survey.
articles.ai_summary:
- A recent survey reveals that landlords and investors are sidelining first-time and local buyers in the UK property market.
- Concerns grow as rising property prices fueled by investors make homeownership increasingly unattainable for newcomers.

articles.title: Next government must stem landlord flow
articles.summary: Scotland's landlords have called for the next Scottish Government to formally recognise the private rented sector's (PRS) importance with a dedicated strategy.
articles.ai_summary:
- Scottish landlords urge the next government to implement a dedicated strategy acknowledging the crucial role of the private rented sector (PRS).  
- A formal recognition is essential to stem the outflow of landlords and ensure a stable rental market in Scotland.

articles.title: Landlords beware of registration requirement for tax advisor
articles.summary: Conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams are among those who could fall foul of new mandatory HMRC tax advisor registration requirements from May, facing penalties of up to £10,000.
articles.ai_summary:
- Starting in May, conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams must comply with new HMRC tax advisor registration rules, or risk facing penalties up to £10,000.  
- Landlords must ensure their tax advisors are registered to avoid costly fines amid these strict new regulations.

articles.title: Licensing doesn’t lead to higher rents – council’s claim
articles.summary: The council is expecting landlords to pay £694 per property without recovering the cost
articles.ai_summary:
- A UK council asserts that new licensing fees will not result in increased rents for tenants, despite the £694 charge imposed on landlords per property.  
- Landlords are expected to absorb these costs without the ability to recover them, raising concerns about financial viability.

articles.title: Labour makes it clear – no rent controls
articles.summary: The pledge was made by Matthew Pennycook
articles.ai_summary:
- Labour's Matthew Pennycook confirms the party will not implement rent controls in the UK.
- The decision highlights a commitment to market-driven housing strategies amidst rising rental challenges.

articles.title: Authority wants to stop “HMO landlord free for all”
articles.summary: The council is also drawing up detailed HMO guidelines
articles.ai_summary:
- Council aims to regulate the chaotic housing situation by creating strict guidelines for HMOs (Houses in Multiple Occupation).
- Plan seeks to curb the "HMO landlord free-for-all" to enhance tenant safety and community standards.

articles.title: Tenants tutored on maximising Renters Rights Act powers
articles.summary: The week-long campaign begins on Monday
articles.ai_summary:
- A week-long initiative kicks off Monday, aimed at educating tenants on how to leverage the Renters Rights Act effectively.  
- The campaign focuses on empowering renters to understand and assert their rights in the current housing landscape.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act provoking spike in S21 evictions
articles.summary: This is exactly what Paul Shamplina predicted
articles.ai_summary:
- The new Renters Rights Act is leading to a significant increase in Section 21 evictions, confirming predictions by property expert Paul Shamplina.  
- Landlords are reacting swiftly to the changes in legislation, prompting concerns about rising displacement among tenants.

articles.title: Rents rising ahead of Renters Rights Act – new data
articles.summary: The data comes from the lettings agency Hamptons
articles.ai_summary:
- Rents are climbing as landlords adjust ahead of the upcoming Renters Rights Act, according to new data from lettings agency Hamptons.  
- The surge signals potential market shifts as tenants prepare for changes in rental regulations.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act will kill off the hobbyist landlord – opinion
articles.summary: We're just 10 days away from the Renters Rights Act becoming law
articles.ai_summary:
- The upcoming Renters Rights Act, set to be enacted in just 10 days, is poised to significantly impact small-scale, hobbyist landlords in the UK.
- Critics argue that the new legislation will drive these landlords out of the market, which could exacerbate the rental crisis and reduce housing availability.

articles.title: Gen Z entrepreneurs turn to property investment
articles.summary: Despite the red tape and extra tax poured on property investors in recent years.
articles.ai_summary:
- Gen Z entrepreneurs are increasingly entering the property investment market, showcasing resilience despite regulatory hurdles and additional taxes.  
- This trend highlights a shift in attitudes towards property investment among younger generations, aiming for long-term financial growth.

articles.title: Tough market for sellers, claims house buying group
articles.summary:
Sellers forced to cut asking prices, endure failed sales or withdraw from the market, reveals House Buyer Bureau’s Chris Hodgkinson.
The post Tough market for sellers, claims house buying group appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Sellers are facing challenges in the UK housing market, leading to price cuts, failed sales, or withdrawals, according to House Buyer Bureau’s Chris Hodgkinson.
- The current landscape is proving tough for sellers as they navigate reduced demand and increased competition.

articles.title: HMRC ‘ramps up’ scrutiny of property valuations for Inheritance Tax
articles.summary:
The number of cases referred to the Valuation Office Agency (VOA) is increasing, boosting Inheritance Tax receipts.
The post HMRC ‘ramps up’ scrutiny of property valuations for Inheritance Tax appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- HMRC is intensifying its examination of property valuations for Inheritance Tax, leading to a rise in cases sent to the Valuation Office Agency (VOA).  
- This surge in scrutiny is contributing to increased receipts from Inheritance Tax.

articles.title: Sleeping tenant caught on camera in £135k property listing photos
articles.summary:
The tenant is seen asleep in bed in the property listing photographs and video for a terrace property for sale in Penrith, Cumbria.
The post Sleeping tenant caught on camera in £135k property listing photos appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- A tenant was unexpectedly captured sleeping in bed during a property listing photo shoot for a £135,000 terrace home in Penrith, Cumbria.  
- The footage, which includes video and images, has gone viral, highlighting an unusual scenario in real estate marketing.

articles.title: Referral fees are a ‘hidden tax’ on homebuyers
articles.summary:
Referral fees have operated as an “unseen surcharge’ on homebuyers, according to the Conveyancing Task Force.
The post Referral fees are a ‘hidden tax’ on homebuyers appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Referral fees are branded as an "unseen surcharge," burdening homebuyers with extra costs, according to the Conveyancing Task Force.  
- This practice is being highlighted as a 'hidden tax' that adds financial strain during the homebuying process.

articles.title: Lomond expands operation north of the border
articles.summary:
David Alexander, of Lomond, says Clyde Property Group will run its portfolio in the West of Scotland.
The post Lomond expands operation north of the border appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Lomond expands its operations into Scotland with Clyde Property Group managing its portfolio in the West.  
- David Alexander announces the strategic move, enhancing Lomond's presence in the Scottish property market.

articles.title: Foxtons’ sales revenue plummets 35%
articles.summary:
Geopolitical headwinds have meant that new buyer activity was lower than expected, says Foxtons’ CEO Guy Gittins.
The post Foxtons’ sales revenue plummets 35% appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Foxtons reports a staggering 35% drop in sales revenue amid unexpected low buyer activity.  
- CEO Guy Gittins cites geopolitical challenges as a key factor impacting the property's market dynamics.

articles.title: Inflation rises as Middle East conflict drives up prices
articles.summary:
Price rises have hit fuel and transport hardest but are expected to soon spread to the rest of the economy.
The post Inflation rises as Middle East conflict drives up prices appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Inflation is spiking due to rising fuel and transport costs, largely driven by the Middle East conflict.  
- These price increases are anticipated to spread throughout the broader UK economy soon.

articles.title: Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market
articles.summary:
The reality is that many new homes being built will never reach the market, says Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, Chief Executive of Alto.
The post Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Market Bypass:** Nine out of ten new homes are sold off-market, indicating a significant gap in availability for buyers.  
- **Industry Insight:** Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, CEO of Alto, highlights the growing trend of properties not reaching the open sales market.
